snippet.title: 🛒 Costco’s CEO was a Forklift Driver? #costco #forklift #ceo
snippet.description:
Costco's new CEO started out as a forklift driver?

With outgoing CEO Craig Jelinek stepping out at the end of the year, Ron Vachris replaces him as the new CEO of Costco. Ron Vachris started out as a forklift driver over 40 years ago at Price Club before it merged with Costco in 1982. He was working at Costco part-time as he studying at Glendale Community College, literally starting from the bottom.

Ron Vachris stayed on and in 1989 became assistant general manager of the store. By 1991, he became a general manager at Costco's first Denver store, from forklift manager to general manager of the store in ten years. By 2010, he was president of the Northwest region of Costco, home to Costco's home base in Seattle. In 2015, he became senior vice president and led the real estate development team, and in 2016 he became head of merchandizing.

And in 2022, he was announced as the CEO of Costco, becoming Costco's No. 2. And in January 2024, Ron Vachris will become the CEO of Costco, the pure definition of making your way up in a company from forklift driver to CEO in 40 years.

#costco #ceo #forklift #forkliftcertified

snippet.title: Walmart CEO Warns Americans Will Freak Out This Winter As Prices Continue To Soar
snippet.description:
Americans are about to hit a breaking point, according to Walmart’s CEO. Soaring prices are severely impacting consumer spending habits, and the world’s biggest retailer is worried about how this trend is going to affect its bottom line this fall and winter. Bill Simon, the head of U.S. operations for four years, revealed that for the first time in a decade, the company is seeing a significant slowdown in sales activity as consumers start to buckle. 
Simon believes the U.S. consumer is on the verge of folding due to an accumulation of pressures exacerbating people’s money issues. During an interview with CNBC earlier this week, the executive pointed to a series of factors working together to squeeze Americans’ purchasing power, and forcing them to tighten their budgets even further. Those include inflation, high-interest rates, and rising political tensions around the nation and the globe.
The former Walmart chief executive also warned that discounts and bargains will become harder to find in the coming weeks and months. Simon said that promotions won’t be nearly as good as they were in the past. He also exposed that not only Walmart but many other major retailers are using an old trick to hide the true value of discounts from consumers.
On top of that, brands are also using shrinkflation by reducing the amount of product in the packaging while keeping the same price. Although some consumers may fall for the trick a few times, eventually they notice the change and realize they are spending more money, so they tend to pull back.
Walmart customers are also outraged about recent price increases. A new viral TikTok exposing how much prices at Walmart stores have climbed is leaving superfans particularly frustrated. TikTok creator @__curtdogg recently racked up 3.7 million views on a video where he compared a 2020 receipt and a recent receipt with the exact same items. The most shocking finding was that even Walmart’s house brand Great Value has shot up in price over the past three years. 
Walmart is already trying to prepare shareholders for an earnings miss this quarter. Even though people are spending more, they are buying less and this is driving sales volumes down and hurting the megaretailer’s bottom line. Walmart executives seem so desperate to conceal the results that, last week, they released a note saying that weight loss medications such as Ozempic are leading consumers to buy less food, and that is eating at the retail giant’s food profit margins. The company said it analyzed anonymous data of the customers who are buying the medicines at their stores, plus what the individuals spend on groceries, and it found that they are consuming increasingly less with each passing month. 
But while only 14% of Americans use Ozempic, nearly 60% said they are struggling to afford groceries due to higher prices, a new report by Attest showed. The situation is getting so extreme that more and more people are losing hope that things will ever get better. And when it comes to incomes, U.S. households making under $50,000 and over $100,000 are all reporting greater financial challenges this year. In essence, they are on an unsustainable path of losing savings, and increasing debt. And with the cost of essentials shooting up all across the nation, it is easy to understand why the U.S. consumer is starting to break.

snippet.title: The History of Costco Wholesale Warehouse.
snippet.description:
Costco Wholesale Corporation (commonly shortened to Costco) is an American multinational corporation which operates a chain of membership-only big-box retail stores (warehouse club).[4] As of 2022, Costco is the fifth largest retailer in the world[5] and is the world's largest retailer of choice and prime beef, organic foods, rotisserie chicken, and wine as of 2016.[6] Costco is ranked #11 on the Fortune 500 rankings of the largest United States corporations by total revenue.[7]
Costco's worldwide headquarters are in Issaquah, Washington, an eastern suburb of Seattle, although its Kirkland Signature house label bears the name of its former location in Kirkland. The company opened its first warehouse (the chain's term for its retail outlets) in Seattle in 1983.[8][9] Through mergers, however, Costco's corporate history dates back to 1976, when its former competitor Price Club was founded in San Diego, California.[10][11][12] As of February 2023, Costco has 855 warehouses worldwide:[1] 587 in the United States, 107 in Canada, 40 in Mexico, 32 in Japan, 29 in the United Kingdom, 18 in Korea, 15 in Australia, 14 in Taiwan, four in Spain, four in China, two in France, and one each in Iceland, New Zealand, and Sweden.

localized.title: The History of Costco Wholesale Warehouse.
localized.description:
Costco Wholesale Corporation (commonly shortened to Costco) is an American multinational corporation which operates a chain of membership-only big-box retail stores (warehouse club).[4] As of 2022, Costco is the fifth largest retailer in the world[5] and is the world's largest retailer of choice and prime beef, organic foods, rotisserie chicken, and wine as of 2016.[6] Costco is ranked #11 on the Fortune 500 rankings of the largest United States corporations by total revenue.[7]
Costco's worldwide headquarters are in Issaquah, Washington, an eastern suburb of Seattle, although its Kirkland Signature house label bears the name of its former location in Kirkland. The company opened its first warehouse (the chain's term for its retail outlets) in Seattle in 1983.[8][9] Through mergers, however, Costco's corporate history dates back to 1976, when its former competitor Price Club was founded in San Diego, California.[10][11][12] As of February 2023, Costco has 855 warehouses worldwide:[1] 587 in the United States, 107 in Canada, 40 in Mexico, 32 in Japan, 29 in the United Kingdom, 18 in Korea, 15 in Australia, 14 in Taiwan, four in Spain, four in China, two in France, and one each in Iceland, New Zealand, and Sweden.

snippet.title: Unleashing the Provocative Charms of Elfriede Jelinek Nobel Prize - Winning 'The Piano Teacher
snippet.description:
2004 Nobel Prize in Literature Winners

"The Piano Teacher" by Elfriede Jelinek is a provocative and unsettling exploration of the dark and repressed desires of a middle-aged piano teacher in Vienna. Through her story, Jelinek examines themes of power, control, gender, and sexuality in a haunting and thought-provoking manner. Reading this novel can challenge your perceptions and make you question the social norms and expectations we live with. It is a must-read for anyone interested in complex character studies and profound psychological explorations of the human condition.

Amazon https://a.co/d/2ZA3mMm

snippet.title: Carter Bays und Bibiana Beglau sind »Freunde von Freunden«
snippet.description:
Carter Bays und Craig Thomas produzierten eine der erfolgsreichsten Sitcoms dieses Jahrtausends. Insgesamt liefen 9 Staffeln von »How I Met Your Mother«. Mit insgesamt 208 Folgen. Das Spin-off »How How I Met Your Father« feierte 2022 in den USA Premiere und läuft seit Februar 2023 im deutschen Free TV. Als gefeierter Drehbuchschreiber weiß er am besten: Pointen müssen sitzen und das ganz schnell hintereinander. So ein bisschen wie beim Stuhltanz. Bei »Freunde von Freunden« dachte Carter Bays kurz an Serie - ohne Frage, schreit jede einzelne Figur nach »Kamera läuft und Action«, aber dann packte ihn das Romanfieber, das Handy klingelte und brachte noch was ganz anderes in ihm zum Vibrieren. In unserem Geistesblüten Magazin No. 20 erzählt er, ob er selber mal Gott spielen oder dem Teufel die Streichhölzer wegnehmen wollte. 

Ist doch klar, dass wir da nachhaken wollten! Zur Deutschlandpremiere seines Romans in den Geistesblüten flog Carter Bays nach Berlin. Er und die Theaterwucht Bibiana Beglau waren das perfekte Match. Kaum lesen sie uns in den New Yorker Central Park, knallt´s und funkt´s.
Im Mittelpunkt von »Freunde von Freunden« steht die 28-jährige New Yorkerin Alice Quick. Als moderne Frau lässt sich Alice rund um die Uhr von ihrem Smartphone informieren. Von der Verlobung ihrer Mitbewohnerin Kelly erfährt sie auf Facebook. Gutmütig wie sie ist, zieht Alice aus und bei Roxy, der Assistentin des Bürgermeisters, ein. In dem »Keller, der versucht eine Wohnung zu sein«, will sie sich endlich zum Medizinertest anmelden. Ist aber vollkommen blockiert. Plötzlich brennt die Bude, ihr Bruder macht einen auf Buddhist und Online-Flirt Bob entpuppt sich als Polyamorist.  

Der Film ist ein Mitschnitt der Buchpremiere. Den Roman »Freunde von Freunden« gibt es hier: https://geistesblueten.buchkatalog.de/freunde-von-freunden-9783550202179

snippet.title: Finally funded with APEX using supply and demand strategy.
snippet.description: I was on the fence about using a prop firm to continue my trading career. I eventually made the jump as it seemed like a good idea to use company money over my own for awhile. Im still your average person working a 9-5 and sometimes our hard earned funds just isnt enough. Also risking your own capital can be extremely stressful.
